project.name: aura-sqm
project.uiScope: SSH terminal user interface and Prometheus metrics, no browser UI in the first build
project.targetOperator: router admin tuning latency-sensitive gaming traffic

conceptualAnchor.name: Latency Oscilloscope
conceptualAnchor.anchorReference: a live oscilloscope used to tune a control signal
conceptualAnchor.specificReferencePoint: stable trace, threshold line, signal health, and damping response
conceptualAnchor.rationale: Aura-SQM is a real-time control daemon. The UI should make latency signal quality and shaper response easy to inspect over SSH.
conceptualAnchor.signatureMotion: readable periodic refresh with stable layout and no decorative animation
conceptualAnchor.typographicDecision: fixed-width numeric fields for rates and latency, short status labels for state, plain text headings for scan speed

derivedTokenLogic.color: use terminal-safe status roles only: normal, warning, failure, and muted metadata
derivedTokenLogic.spacing: compact rows with stable columns so changing values do not shift the layout
derivedTokenLogic.typography: monospace-first because the TUI runs in SSH terminals and displays numeric telemetry
derivedTokenLogic.motion: refresh cadence communicates live state; no extra animation is required

motionPaletteDecision.palette: terminal-safe status colors with text labels so color is never the only signal
motionPaletteDecision.spatialDecision: no 3D or canvas because the first UI is terminal-based and must run over SSH

libraryDecisions.purpose: TUI rendering after daemon state model exists
libraryDecisions.fallbackIfUnavailable: plain terminal output with fixed-width text
